component.name: TextField
component.description: A single-line text input for capturing short, free-form values such as names, emails, or search terms. Wraps a label, a bordered white input container (with optional leading/trailing icons), and an optional helper message that can carry neutral, error, or success status.
component.note: Label is 12/16 semibold neutral.600; input container is white with a 1px neutral.300 border and SHARP corners (radius-none, DS convention), 40px tall (32px compact). Hover/focus are real CSS states (no `state` prop), matching Button/Checkbox. The helper message has three tones; error wins over success. Synced to Figma '❖ Text field'.

tokens.focusRing: 2px colors.border.primary.strong (forest.700), offset 1px, on the container; switches to danger.700 when invalid. The inner input has outline:none.

useCases: Capture a short single-line value — name, email, username, URL
Search input within a toolbar or page header
A field inside a form section with a label, helper text, and validation
Numeric or formatted entry via the forwarded `type` attribute
Input with a leading or trailing icon (e.g. a search glyph or a clear affordance)

commonPatterns.name: Labeled form field
commonPatterns.description: Standard field with a label and placeholder inside a form.
commonPatterns.jsx: <TextField label='Full name' placeholder='Jane Doe' value={name} onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)} />

commonPatterns.name: Required field with validation
commonPatterns.description: Required field that surfaces an error message + warning icon when invalid.
commonPatterns.jsx: <TextField label='Email' type='email' isRequired isInvalid={!!error} helperText={error ?? 'We will never share it.'} />

commonPatterns.name: Success confirmation
commonPatterns.description: Valid tone shows a forest message with a check-circle icon after a passing check.

commonPatterns.name: Search with leading icon
commonPatterns.description: Compact field with a leading search icon, used in a toolbar.

antiPatterns.reason: A single-line input clips and scrolls long text horizontally and offers no line wrapping.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use TextArea for comments, descriptions, or any multi-line entry.

antiPatterns.reason: The input has no accessible name and is unusable by screen readers.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass label, or set aria-label for a standalone field (e.g. a toolbar search).

antiPatterns.scenario: Conveying the error only through the red border
antiPatterns.reason: Color alone fails accessibility; users who can't perceive the color get no error.
antiPatterns.alternative: Pass helperText with isInvalid so the message text, warning icon, and aria role=alert all carry the error.

antiPatterns.reason: Placeholder text disappears on focus/typing, harming recall and accessibility.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always provide a persistent label; use placeholder only for supplementary examples.

slots.label: Optional — text above the input (omit only for standalone fields, then set aria-label)
slots.helperText: Optional helper/status message below the input (the Figma <Message>)

interactions.hover: border darkens to neutral.400 (only when not disabled and not focused)
interactions.focus: focus-within → forest.500 border + 2px forest.700 outline offset 1px on the container; inner input has outline:none
interactions.filled: typed value renders in neutral.800 at 14/20; placeholder is neutral.500
interactions.invalid: danger.500 border in every state; focus ring switches to danger.700; sets aria-invalid; pair with an error helperText
interactions.valid: success tone on the helper message (forest.500 text + check-circle icon); border is unchanged
interactions.disabled: neutral.50 fill, neutral.300 border, neutral.400 text/icons, cursor not-allowed

behavior.toneResolution: Helper tone is error if isInvalid, else success if isValid, else neutral — error always wins over success.
behavior.controlled: Pass value + onChange for controlled; omit value and pass defaultValue for uncontrolled. ref and remaining input attributes (type, name, maxLength, etc.) are forwarded to the native <input>.

accessibility.focusRing: 2px forest.700 outline, offset 1px, on the container (danger.700 when invalid)
accessibility.labelWiring: label is linked to the input via htmlFor/id; a standalone field (no label) must carry aria-label
accessibility.requiredState: isRequired sets the native required attribute (and its implicit aria)
accessibility.describedBy: helperText is linked to the input through aria-describedby; the error message has role=alert
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab moves focus in/out; standard text-editing keys apply
accessibility.screenReader: Native input drives the accessible name from the associated label or aria-label; error messages are announced via role=alert
accessibility.contrastNote: Error MESSAGE text uses danger.700 (5.81:1 on white) so 12px text passes AA; the danger.500 border is a 3:1 non-text element and passes.

athenaConstraints.errorMessageColor: danger.700 (--text-danger-strong) — never danger.500 for the message text

figmaDivergences.figma: explicit state property (default / hover / focus / typing / filled)
figmaDivergences.implementation: real CSS states (:hover, :focus-within) plus the value the user types
figmaDivergences.reason: Matches Button/Checkbox; states are driven by the browser and the input value rather than a prop.
danger.500 is only 3.54:1 on white and fails AA for 12px text; danger.700 is 5.81:1 and passes. The danger BORDER stays danger.500 (a 3:1 non-text element, which passes).

figmaDivergences.figma: not present in the icon set
figmaDivergences.implementation: added a `check-circle` icon for the success message
figmaDivergences.reason: The valid tone needs a 16px confirmation glyph alongside the forest text.

aiHints.context: Reach for TextField to capture a single line of free-form text. Always give it a label (or aria-label). Use isRequired for required fields, isInvalid + an error helperText to surface validation, isValid for success confirmation, and isCompact for dense toolbars. Add iconBefore/iconAfter for affordances like search. For multi-line input use TextArea instead.
neverUseWhen: The input is multi-line — use TextArea
There is no label and no aria-label — the field has no accessible name
An error is shown only via the border color — pair isInvalid with an error helperText
You intend the placeholder to stand in for the label — provide a persistent label
